data.title: Transocean outlines $11B pro forma backlog and targets 1.5x leverage post-Valaris acquisition
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Transocean Ltd. (RIG) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Keelan Adamson, President and CEO, emphasized the company's strong operational and financial execution in 2025, highlighting "solid adjusted EBITDA of $385 million and free cash flow of $321 million" in the fourth quarter, with annual adjusted EBITDA of $1.37 billion and free cash flow of $626 million. Adamson stated, "During the year, we materially strengthened the balance sheet, retiring about $1.3 billion in debt...These actions and the additional debt payments made in 2025 reduced our annual interest expense by nearly $90 million, enhanced our financial flexibility and increased the value of our equity currency, ultimately enabling the recently announced transaction with Valaris."

  • Strategic cost reductions were detailed, with $100 million in costs removed and plans to decrease costs by an additional $150 million in 2026. Adamson noted, "We are leaner, more efficient and more profitable."

  • On the Valaris acquisition, Adamson described it as a "transformational combination" and stated, "We are incredibly excited about the capabilities of our combined business...Our pro forma combined backlog of nearly $11 billion and cash flow-generating capability are expected to accelerate debt reduction, resulting in leverage of around 1.5x within 24 months of closing."

  • Adamson provided a global market update, citing "tendering activity is increasing" and the expectation that "deepwater utilization to move meaningfully higher and to greater than 90% through 2027."

  • R. Vayda, Executive VP & CFO, added, "In the fourth quarter, we generated contract drilling revenues of $1.04 billion at an average daily revenue of approximately $461,000...Adjusted EBITDA was $385 million, implying a very healthy margin of 37% and cash flow from operations was approximately $349 million, a sequential increase of 42%."

Outlook

  • Management expects "free cash flow to be in line with or better than that achieved in 2025" for 2026, despite some anticipated idle time for several rigs, as ongoing cost and interest expense reductions and improved working capital management continue.

  • Vayda stated, "We expect to end 2026 with liquidity of between $1.6 billion and $1.7 billion, which excludes the effect of any incremental opportunistic deleveraging."

  • The company anticipates closing the Valaris transaction in the second half of 2026 and expects leverage to drop to "around 1.5x within 24 months of closing."

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter contract drilling revenues were reported at $1.04 billion, with operating and maintenance expense at $605 million and G&A expense at $50 million.

  • Free cash flow for the quarter was $321 million, with a free cash flow margin of 31%. Liquidity at quarter end was approximately $1.5 billion, including $620 million in unrestricted cash and $510 million in undrawn credit.

  • The CFO noted, "This is the best quarterly free cash flow we have generated in several years and is a direct result of excellent operational performance, execution on our cost savings initiatives, lower cash interest expense and effective management of our working capital."

Q&A

  • Gregory Lewis, BTIG: Asked about post-acquisition chartering strategy and economies of scale. Adamson responded that the combination allows Transocean "to address the necessary cost across the combination...minimize...overlap of cost structure" and improve service provision globally, aiming for "reliable and predictable" execution.

  • Lewis also inquired about the jack-up market. Adamson indicated the company "learned from the past" and sees "a great opportunity for us to learn from the Valaris team that runs that jackup fleet to continue to deliver exceptional performance and incremental cash to the combined entity going forward."

  • Edward Kim, Barclays: Questioned confidence in a late 2026/2027 offshore inflection. Adamson cited customer conversations and "the number of tenders that release," while Roddie Mackenzie, Chief Commercial Officer, pointed to increasing rig awards and a growing number of multiyear programs.

  • Kim also asked about Petrobras blend and extend negotiations. Adamson confirmed guidance already reflects "our best guess based upon the conversations that we've had," with no significant upside assumed.

  • Fredrik Stene, Clarksons: Sought color on fleet placement and regional opportunities. Adamson and Mackenzie emphasized global flexibility and highlighted new tender activity, with Mackenzie noting "no shortage of opportunities" and potential for rigs to shift regions based on demand.

  • Doug Becker, Capital One: Asked about customer feedback on the Valaris deal. Adamson reported "overwhelmingly positive" response, stating customers "understand the need to drive costs out of the business."

  • Becker inquired about a win-win for Petrobras negotiations. Mackenzie explained the focus is on "cost reductions and optimization" and "significant extensions" for the core fleet, aiming to keep rigs busy and improve revenue efficiency.

  • Keith Beckmann, Pickering: Questioned the prospects for seventh-gen rigs and Gulf market assets. Adamson said reactivations would only occur if investment could be recovered, expressing confidence in long-term market strength but not expecting near-term reactivations.

  • Noel Parks, Tuohy Brothers: Asked about late entrants to offshore exploration. Mackenzie described a "pivot back towards oil and gas, particularly offshore and deepwater," with capital discipline driving focus on cost-effective, reliable energy.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, with constructive inquiries about the Valaris acquisition, fleet utilization, and regional market opportunities. Questions were direct without evident skepticism or pressing negativity.

  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone throughout, particularly regarding cost savings, operational execution, and the strategic rationale for the Valaris acquisition. Phrases like "we are incredibly excited" and "we are well positioned to capitalize on improving demand" were prominent.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence was more pronounced, especially in context of the acquisition and strengthened balance sheet. Analysts’ tone remained consistently constructive, reflecting cautious optimism.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter featured the major announcement of the Valaris acquisition, absent in the previous quarter.

  • Guidance language shifted to incorporate expectations from the pending merger and projected synergies, whereas the previous quarter focused on organic cost reductions and fleet rationalization.

  • Management’s tone increased in confidence, reflecting improved free cash flow, operational results, and strategic positioning.

  • Analysts’ focus broadened from utilization and day rates to include consolidation strategy, market opportunities, and synergies.

  • Key metric changes included higher adjusted EBITDA, free cash flow, and reported backlog post-acquisition announcement.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged "near-term moderation in tendering activity," but expects strengthening deepwater outlook.

  • Idle time risk for specific rigs (KG2, Deepwater Proteus, Deepwater Skyros) is reflected in guidance, with upside if contract extensions are secured.

  • Blend and extend negotiations with Petrobras remain a key variable, though management expects any impact is already captured in guidance.

  • The company is focused on "continued cost reduction initiatives" and maintaining financial flexibility to manage industry cycles.

Final Takeaway

Transocean’s management underscored a transformative year marked by operational excellence, aggressive deleveraging, and a pending acquisition that will create a combined entity with nearly $11 billion in backlog. The leadership team is projecting continued strong free cash flow, significant cost reductions, and accelerated debt reduction, targeting leverage of 1.5x within two years of closing the Valaris deal. With a constructive market view underpinned by rising tendering activity and a robust pipeline of multiyear offshore projects, the company positions itself as a leading, resilient player in the evolving offshore drilling sector.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Sensata outlines new three-segment structure and targets at least 20 basis points margin expansion for 2026
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sensata Technologies Holding plc (ST) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Stephan Von Schuckmann highlighted a transformative year, emphasizing operational excellence, capital allocation, and growth as the three pillars driving Sensata’s strategy. He stated, "We reported results at or above the midpoint of our guidance ranges every quarter this year."
  • Von Schuckmann detailed a reorganization into three operating segments—Automotive (57% of 2025 revenue), Industrials (21%), and Aerospace, Defense and Commercial Equipment (22%)—each with a distinct mandate for value creation and growth.
  • He described a focus on leveraging Sensata’s automotive pedigree, expanding into data centers, and capitalizing on super cycle growth in defense and aviation.
  • CFO Andrew Lynch noted, "We reported revenue of $918 million for the fourth quarter of 2025, which exceeded the midpoint of our guidance range by $13 million."
  • Lynch also stated, "We delivered record free cash flow of $490 million in 2025, an increase of 25% compared to 2024 free cash flow of $393 million."

Outlook

  • For Q1 2026, Lynch shared, "We expect first quarter revenue of $917 million to $937 million, adjusted operating income of $168 million to $175 million, adjusted operating margins of 18.4% to 18.6%. Adjusted net income of $118 million to $125 million and adjusted earnings per share of $0.81 to $0.85."
  • He added that full-year 2026 is expected to see low single-digit year-over-year revenue growth, with margins normalizing to 19% or better in Q2 and expanding each quarter thereafter.
  • Management reiterated a margin floor of 19% and targeted margin expansion of at least 20 basis points for 2026.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2025 revenue reached $918 million, a $10 million increase year-over-year, marking the first year-over-year quarterly revenue rise since Q1 2024.
  • Adjusted operating margin stood at 19.6% for Q4, up 30 basis points year-over-year.
  • Adjusted earnings per share for Q4 2025 was $0.88, rising $0.14 year-over-year.
  • 2025 full-year revenue was $3.70 billion, down 6% from 2024, primarily due to divestitures and product life cycle management actions. Adjusted operating margin for 2025 was 19.0%.
  • Free cash flow hit a record $490 million, with a 97% conversion rate.
  • Segment performance showed organic revenue growth across all three segments in Q4: Automotive organic growth at 1%, Industrials at 8%, and Aerospace, Defense and Commercial Equipment at 7%.

Q&A

  • Wamsi Mohan, BofA Securities: Asked about longer-term revenue potential. Von Schuckmann explained that growth opportunities are real and broad across all segments, expressing confidence about growth in 2026 and beyond.
  • Joseph Giordano, TD Cowen: Inquired about the cost and strategy behind the new segmentation. Lynch said added overhead is expected to be offset by growth and margin expansion. Von Schuckmann emphasized the segmentation aligns with value creation and strategic pillars.
  • Mark Delaney (via Vermont), Goldman Sachs: Questioned progress with Asian OEMs. Von Schuckmann cited new business wins in China, Japan, and South Korea, with content per vehicle in South Korea now exceeding North American levels.
  • Robert Jamieson, Vertical Research: Asked whether the new structure allows more nimble reinvestment. Von Schuckmann confirmed this as a key strategic benefit.
  • Joseph Spak, UBS: Queried data center opportunities. Von Schuckmann clarified that existing products fuel organic growth, with new R&D broadening the portfolio, especially in flow sensors and liquid cooling.
  • Luke Junk, Baird: Sought details on data center and defense materiality. Lynch provided segment revenue breakdowns, highlighting highest growth in commercial aviation and defense. Von Schuckmann noted double-digit CAGR expected in UAVs.
  • Manmohanpreet Singh, JPMorgan: Asked about A2L gas leak detection sensors’ impact. Lynch said revenue grew from $10-$15 million in 2024 to $70 million in 2025, and expects a run rate exceeding $100 million.
  • Konstandinos Tasoulis, Wells Fargo: Probed data center value-add. Von Schuckmann described a broad product approach, expecting significant growth potential.
  • Steven Fox, Fox Advisors: Asked about segment margin opportunity. Lynch said margin expansion focus is across all segments, with higher growth in Industrials and Aerospace.
  • Shreyas Patil, Wolfe Research: Noted Q4 Auto organic growth lagged industry. Lynch attributed this to regional mix, with normalization expected in 2026. Von Schuckmann described Sensata’s ability to grow with any propulsion type.
  • Joe Giordano, TD Cowen: Questioned risk of entering new markets. Von Schuckmann outlined rigorous frameworks and standards to mitigate risk, emphasizing use of existing products and production assets.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on growth strategy, regional wins, and the impact of restructuring, with a neutral to slightly positive tone.
  • Management maintained an optimistic and confident tone, especially around growth mandates and market opportunities. Von Schuckmann repeatedly stated confidence in the leadership team and growth potential.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone shifted from cautious optimism to more focused questioning on execution and growth details. Management tone remained consistently confident and structured, with increased emphasis on the new segmentation and growth frameworks.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter featured a significant strategic shift to a three-segment structure, compared to the previous quarter’s focus on operational excellence and cash generation.
  • Guidance language became more explicit, with management reiterating a margin floor of 19% and targeting at least 20 basis points of margin expansion versus prior reaffirmations of defending the margin floor.
  • Analysts in the current quarter concentrated questions on the implications and execution of the new segmentation, Asian market wins, and new growth markets such as data centers and defense, whereas the previous quarter focused more on capital allocation and leadership changes.
  • Management’s confidence remained strong, but the current quarter’s tone was more forward-looking, highlighting structural and strategic changes rather than just operational achievements.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged precious metals pricing as a headwind for 2026 but stated, "We expect to offset any precious metals headwinds through a combination of supply chain optimization, product redesign and pass-through of these costs to our customers."
  • Pressure from tariffs continues, but mitigation strategies from 2025 are being used for metals inflation.
  • Analysts questioned risk of overextension into new markets, with management responding that rigorous frameworks and use of core products reduce risk.

Final Takeaway

Sensata Technologies emphasized a substantial transformation for 2025, capped by a reorganization into three operating segments, each with distinct growth mandates. Management remains confident in the ability to deliver low single-digit revenue growth and at least 20 basis points margin expansion in 2026, supported by robust free cash flow, disciplined capital allocation, and a diversified set of market opportunities, particularly in Automotive, Industrials, and Aerospace, Defense and Commercial Equipment. The leadership team asserts that the foundation laid in 2025 positions Sensata for continued value creation and growth in the coming years.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: AI-related stocks edge higher, led by networking, while CoreWeave drags hyperscalers down
data.description:
Artificial intelligence-related stocks, along with the broader market, trended up during Friday trading after the U.S. Supreme Court's decision to strike down President Donald Trump's tariffs.

Several networking stocks, particularly those focused on optical products, were showing the strongest gains. Lumentum (LITE) and Ciena (CIEN) had both increased by 6%. Corning (GLW) and Coherent (COHR) had both jumped 7%. Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) had popped 11%.

Applied Optoelectronics is in the process of building a 210,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in Sugar Land, Texas.

CoreWeave drags down hyperscalers

Several of the most popular hyperscaler stocks were not responding positively during Friday trading. Oracle (ORCL), Nebius (NBIS), and Applied Digital (APLD) had all declined by about 4%. IREN (IREN) had inched down 0.6%. CoreWeave (CRWV) had plunged 9%.

CoreWeave is facing a class action lawsuit over alleged data center delays and concealed infrastructure risks. The suit is being led by the law firm Hagens Berman, which specializes in shareholder rights.

"We are investigating the alleged gap between the company's assurances of its growth trajectory and the alleged reality of systemic construction delays at its primary data center sites," said Reed Kathrein, the Hagens Berman partner leading the firm's investigation.

Chipmakers show mixed action

The most significant names among chipmakers were demonstrating mixed action. AMD (AMD) was down 0.6%, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) perked up 2%, Intel (INTC) dipped 3%, and Nvidia (NVDA) inched up 0.4%.

The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) had increased 1%, surpassing the larger indices in daily gains. The S&P 500 Index (SP500) had ticked up 0.5%, the NASDAQ Composite Index (COMP:IND) edged up 0.8%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average Index (DJI) was up 0.2%.

Meanwhile, Rackspace (RXT) continued the surge it started earlier this week after partnering with Palantir (PLTR) to help increase the speed at which enterprises can deploy and operate its analytical and AI-driven software. It had soared more than 40% by noon trading, and its stock price has now more than quadrupled since Monday.

data.title: Compass stock slips after Sen. Warren questions DOJ review of Anywhere deal
data.description:
Compass (COMP) stock fell 2.4% in Friday midday trading following a report that Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) questioned the Justice Department about its review of Compass's Anywhere Real Estate acquisition.

The transaction was completed in January, less than four months after the $10B all-stock merger agreement was announced. On the same day that the deal closed, the Wall Street Journal reported that the head of the DOJ's antitrust division wanted an extended review of the real estate brokerage deal but was overruled by senior officials. 

Bloomberg News reported on Friday that Sen. Warren wrote a letter to U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi seeking more information about the review.

Sen. Warren's office didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Lucid Group to lay off 12% of workforce -- report
data.description:
Lucid Motors (LCID) is reportedly cutting 12% of its workforce to improve profitability even as the company increases production of its Gravity SUV and plans to launch a $50K mid-sized EV later this year. 

According to an internal memo viewed by TechCrunch, California-based Lucid (LCID) said the job cuts aim to “improve operational effectiveness and optimize our resources as we continue on our path toward profitability.”

While the cuts could impact more than 800 Lucid (LCID) employees, those working in manufacturing, logistics, and quality are reportedly not affected.

The company said it will provide severance, bonuses, continued health benefits, and transition services to support those employees who will be laid off. Costs associated with the separations were not disclosed. 

Lucid (LCID) shares are down again on Friday, extending its losing streak to a fourth straight day and adding to its 70% year-over-year loss.

data.title: Celestica's growth, engineering capabilities should widen its opportunities: BNP
data.description:
Celestica (CLS) shares have been hit hard recently amid the continued perceived artificial intelligence-induced disruption. However, BNP Paribas believes the company's growth and engineering prowess could be a buying opportunity for investors.

“We believe Celestica's growing capacity and engineering capabilities widens the company's [Original Design Manufacturer] networking opportunity and margin expansion potential,” BNP analyst Karl Ackerman wrote in a note to clients after a meeting with the company's CFO and investor relations team. “CLS remains a top idea.”

Delving deeper, Ackerman said the company's original design manufacturing capabilities likely helped it see customer orders in 2025 that were “multiples” ahead of where they have been in prior years. As such, there is the belief that 2026 could see a similar response. 

Additionally, Ackerman said that new orders typically take around three years to turn into material sales, so the $1B in capex spending this year should help boost growth in 2027, 2028, and 2029. He also said he believes Celestica has won two follow-on programs for Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) tensor processing units, which should start to ramp up in 2027, notable given that Google could see external demand for its TPUs.

Other potential catalysts include the 1.6T networking switch programs across the three major hyperscalers that will start to hit in the second-half of 2026 and throughout 2027; its ability to compete in co-packaged optics for switch opportunities; the company's Digital Native program, which is on track to ramp in the first-half of 2027; and the fact it is likely engaged with multiple Ethernet for Scale-Up Networking opportunities. 

“We see multiple tailwinds to support 40%+ revenue growth and margin expansion through CY28 for Celestica, such as ramping server and switch programs at hyperscalers, broadening opportunities with digital natives, a modest recovery in enterprise networking, and new semicap business,” Ackerman added. “Celestica is poised to benefit from burgeoning data center capex and increasing demand for customized rack-level AI systems and networking switches to support AI workloads.”

data.title: Five9 surges after Q4 beat
data.description:
Shares of Five9 (FIVN) surged about 13% on Friday after fourth quarter results beat estimates.

Q4 Metrics

For the fourth quarter ended Dec. 31, 2025, the company's revenue jumped about 8% year-over-year to $300.28M. Non-GAAP EPS rose about 1.3% year-over-year to $0.80. Both bottom and top line numbers surpassed analysts' estimates.

"We're pleased with our fourth quarter performance, exiting the year with a revenue run rate of $1.2 billion and adjusted EBITDA margin of 26%. The CX market is undergoing a significant transformation as AI becomes central to customer experience," said Chairman Mike Burkland. 

Five9 — which provides cloud software for contact centers — saw ull year 2025 total revenue increase 10% to a record $1.149B, according to the company. 

Outlook

For the first quarter of 2026, Five9 expects revenue between $296.5M and $302.5M (midpoint at $299.5M) versus a consensus revenue estimate of $300.00M. The company expects non-GAAP EPS in the range of $0.66 to $0.70 (midpoint at $0.68) compared to consensus EPS estimate of $0.68.

For the full year 2026, Five9 expects revenue to be in the range of $1.247B to $1.261B (midpoint at $1.254B) versus a consensus revenue estimate of $1.26B. The company expects non-GAAP EPS to be between $3.15 and $3.21 (midpoint at $3.18) compared to a consensus EPS estimate of $3.18.

data.title: Regenxbio dealt win by appeals court in patent spat with Sarepta
data.description:
• A federal appeals court has reversed a lower court ruling that found in favor of Sarepta Therapeutics (SRPT) in a patent dispute with Regenxbio (RGNX).
  • The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit found that a Delaware federal court decision rendered in January 2024 on the patent was incorrect as claims related to it are "not directed to a natural phenomenon."
  • The case has now been remanded for further proceedings. 
  • The patent in question, 10,526,617 ('617), relates to technology behind Regenxbio's adeno-associated virus (AAV) gene delivery platform. The company claims that Sarepta uses this patent in the manufacture of its Duchenne muscular dystrophy treatment Elevidys (delandistrogene moxeparvovec).
  • Regenxbio has an exclusive license to the '617 patent from the University of Pennsylvania, which is also a plaintiff in the case.

data.title: Dr. Reddy’s biosimilar for Bristol’s Orencia undergoes FDA review
data.description:
Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories (RDY) announced on Friday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has accepted for review its marketing application for a biosimilar targeting Orencia, a blockbuster arthritis therapy marketed by Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY).

If approved, the product named DRL_AB will be available as an intravenous infusion for adults with inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis and psoriatic arthritis, the company said.

The Indian drugmaker had submitted the 351 ((k)) Biologics License Application for DRL_AB in December, becoming the first to pursue U.S. approval for an off-patent version of Orencia (abatacept).

The company seeks interchangeable status for the drug, meaning if approved, DRL_AB could be substituted at the pharmacy level without the intervention of the healthcare provider who first prescribed the reference product.

In 2025, Bristol-Myers (BMY) generated $2.7B in revenue from Orencia in the U.S., indicating a ~1% YoY decline as lower selling prices partly offset higher demand.

data.title: Western Union stock drops after Q4 revenue disappoints
data.description:
Western Union (WU) stock fell 6.1% in Friday morning trading after the company's Q4 revenue fell short of consensus, even though its 2026 earnings guidance was largely in line with Wall Street expectations.

The company known for wire transfers of cash expects 2026 adjusted EPS  of $1.75-$1.85 (midpoint $1.80 vs. $1.79 consensus) and full-year revenue growth of 5%-8%, implying $4.25B-$4.37B (midpoint $4.31B vs. the $4.12B consensus).

Q4 adjusted EPS of $0.45, beating the average analyst estimate of $0.43, fell from $0.47 in Q3 and increased from $0.40 in Q4 2024. 

Q4 revenue of $1.01B, trailing the $1.04B consensus, decreased from $1.03B in the prior quarter and $1.06B a year ago. Q4 adjusted revenue, excluding Iraq, was $1.00B in Q4 2025, down from $1.02B in Q3 and $1.03B in Q4 2024.

"Despite a challenging operating environment in 2025, we delivered meaningful progress across the business," said President and CEO Devin McGranahan. "We strengthened our Consumer Services offerings, expanded our owned retail footprint, and accelerated our transition to a more digital-first operating model."

Consumer money transfer revenue of $871.5M fell 7% Y/Y, while consumer services revenue of $136.9M increased 15%. Branded digital revenue, which makes up 30% of consumer money transfer revenue, increased 7% Y/Y on a reported basis.

Western Union's (WU) consumer services business's growth reflects the expansion of its Travel Money business, which included the acquisition of Eurochange Limited, and higher revenues from the company's bill pay business.
